# Terms of Service

**Effective Date:** 03/26/2026

These Terms of Service ("Terms") govern your use of the Osprey website, the hosted Osprey proxy service at
[api.osprey.ac](https://api.osprey.ac), and related hosted materials or services made available by the Osprey project
that are not expressly governed by a separate open-source license (collectively, the "Service").

By using the Service, you agree to these Terms. If you do not agree, please do not use the Service.

## 1. About Osprey

Osprey is an open-source browser protection project intended to help identify potentially malicious, phishing,
scam-related, or otherwise unsafe websites.

Osprey is meant to supplement your own judgment and other security tools, not replace them.

## 2. Open-Source Software

The Osprey browser extension, OspreyProxy, and related source code made available by the project are licensed under the
GNU General Public License v3.0 ("GPLv3"), unless a different license is expressly stated for a specific component or
file.

Your rights to use, copy, modify, and distribute project source code are governed by the applicable open-source
license, not by these Terms.

If there is any conflict between these Terms and an applicable open-source license with respect to software released
under that license, the open-source license will control for that software.

## 3. GitHub

Project source code, releases, issue tracking, and related materials may be hosted or distributed through GitHub. Your
use of GitHub is governed by GitHub's own terms, policies, and privacy practices, not by these Terms.

## 4. The Free Version

The current free version of Osprey is made available on a non-commercial basis for end users, researchers, and
organizations using it for their own internal purposes.

We may introduce a separate paid or enterprise offering in the future. If we do, that offering may be subject to
separate or additional terms.

## 5. Commercial Use of the Hosted Service

The hosted Osprey proxy service and any public APIs made available through it are provided for the project's free
version and related non-commercial use.

You may not use the hosted Service or its APIs to operate, power, resell, redistribute, or provide a commercial
product or service without prior written permission from the Osprey project. This includes using the hosted Service as
part of a paid offering, charging others for access to Osprey-backed lookups, or offering Osprey-derived API results
as a commercial service.

This restriction applies to the hosted Service and APIs, not to any source code made available under GPLv3. Rights in
GPLv3-licensed code remain governed by the applicable open-source license.

## 6. No Guarantee of Detection

Osprey is a security aid only. We do not guarantee that the Service will detect, block, or correctly classify every
malicious, phishing, scam-related, deceptive, or otherwise unsafe site or URL.

Threat intelligence can be incomplete, delayed, unavailable, or inaccurate, and third-party providers may affect the
coverage or availability of the Service. You are responsible for your own security decisions and for how you use the
results provided by Osprey.

## 7. Acceptable Use

Please do not use the Service to:

- violate applicable law;
- interfere with or disrupt the Service or its infrastructure;
- bypass security controls or rate limits;
- send abusive, excessive, or malformed automated traffic;
- use the hosted Service or its APIs to operate, resell, or support a commercial product or service without prior
  written permission; or
- use the Service to support harmful, fraudulent, or abusive activity.

We may rate-limit, restrict, or block access to the hosted Service if necessary to protect the Service, users, or
third parties.

## 8. Third-Party Services

The Service may rely on third-party threat intelligence, DNS filtering, hosting, network, security, or infrastructure
providers. We are not responsible for outages, errors, policy changes, or inaccuracies caused by third-party services.

## 9. Privacy

Our [Privacy Policy](./PRIVACY.md) describes how the current Osprey service processes data.

If we introduce additional products or an enterprise offering in the future, those services may be governed by a
separate privacy policy or supplemental notice.

## 10. Project Name and Branding

Except to the extent governed by applicable open-source licenses, the Osprey name, logos, branding, website content,
and other non-code materials made available by the project may be protected by applicable law.

These Terms do not give you permission to use the Osprey name, logos, branding, or other source identifiers in a way
that is likely to cause confusion about affiliation, sponsorship, or endorsement.

## 11. Changes to the Service

The Service may change over time, and we may modify, limit, suspend, or discontinue parts of it at any time.

We may also update these Terms from time to time. The Effective Date above reflects the date of the most recent
revision. Continued use of the Service after an update means you accept the revised Terms.

THE SERVICE IS PROVIDED "AS IS" AND "AS AVAILABLE," WITHOUT WARRANTIES OF ANY KIND, TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY

TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY LAW, WE DISCLAIM WARRANTIES OF MERCHANTABILITY, FITNESS FOR A PARTICULAR PURPOSE,
